entries.content: Real compensation = nominal compensation / CPI. Seven columns with complex provenance: S008A (Sahr/MeasuringWorth CPI 1780-2010), S008B (US CPI 1982-84=100, Sahr 1780-1785 + MeasuringWorth 1786-2010), S008C (BLS Manufacturing Productivity output/hr index 1958=100), S008D (BLS manufacturing output/hr in 1958 dollars), S008E (BLS real unit labor cost 1889=100), S008F (BLS real compensation 1889=100), S008 (final: Mfg production worker real compensation = comp/hr / CPI, 1790-2010).

MeasuringWorth.com. US Consumer Price Index, 1982-84=100. Sahr provides inflation conversion factors for 1780-1785; MeasuringWorth extends 1786-2010. Officer & Williamson (2025) citation for MeasuringWorth CPI. BLS Manufacturing Productivity series: output per hour, unit labor costs, and real hourly compensation, 1889-2010.

Fig 2.6 plots manufacturing real worker compensation against productivity. The series shows real compensation (compensation per hour deflated by CPI) from 1790-2010. The 7-column structure reflects the multi-source construction: CPI from Sahr/MeasuringWorth, BLS productivity indices, and the derived real compensation series. Extension covers CPI only (S008A/B) via MeasuringWorth 2011-2024; BLS columns (S008C-F) extendable via pull_bls_ch02.py.

citations.full: Sahr, R. and MeasuringWorth. Inflation conversion factors, 1780-2010. US CPI 1982-84=100. Sahr (1780-1785), MeasuringWorth (1786-2010).
Officer, L.H. and Williamson, S.H. (2025). MeasuringWorth - US Consumer Price Index. https://www.measuringworth.com
U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Manufacturing Productivity and Costs. Output per hour, real unit labor cost, real hourly compensation. Series PRS30006092, PRS30006112, PRS30006152.

methodology_summary: S008 constructs manufacturing production worker real compensation as compensation per hour divided by CPI. CPI combines Sahr (1780-1785) and MeasuringWorth (1786-2010), both 1982-84=100. BLS provides manufacturing productivity (output/hr, 1958=100), output per hour in 1958 dollars, real unit labor cost (1889=100), and real compensation (1889=100) for 1889-2010. Final series S008 = Mfg real compensation = comp/hr / CPI, 1790-2010. Extension: MeasuringWorth CPI only (2011-2024); BLS manufacturing columns extendable via BLS API.
known_issues: Row 1 metadata concatenated from original Excel merged cells - complex provenance
S008A uses Sahr for 1780-1785 only; full Sahr dataset at Inputs/Sahr/ cross-referenced in ANU_CHOPPED_CATALOG
BLS columns (S008C-F) require BLS API key for extension

cross_references.relationship: Complements S007 - S008 is real compensation (wages), S007 is productivity; together show wage-productivity gap
Shared CPI source - MeasuringWorth used for both GDP and CPI
